hypothesis: If a hybrid deep learning architecture combining a 2D CNN for surface encoding, a GRU for polymer sequence encoding, and a multi-head attention mechanism to model their interaction is trained on high-throughput MD-derived binding free energies, then it will more accurately predict polymer-surface adhesion energies than baseline models using simpler or single-modality encoders, because the attention mechanism explicitly captures the sequence-specific, position-dependent interactions between polymer beads and heterogeneous surface patterns that govern binding affinity.
